subject: Discrete Random Variables
question: Under an insurance policy, a maximum of five claims may be filed per year by a policyholder. Let $p_n$ be the probability that a policyholder files $n$ claims during a given year, where $n = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5$. An actuary makes the following observations: (i) $p_n \ge p_{n+1}$ for $0 \le n \le 4$ (ii) The difference between $p_n$ and $p_{n+1}$ is the same for $0 \le n \le 4$ (iii) Exactly 40% of policyholders file fewer than two claims during a given year. Calculate the probability that a random policyholder will file more than three claims during a given year.
